To extract information from text, it is necessary to design structure for it. This may involve: 
i.	Translating the text to a preferred language.
ii.	Extracting and normalizing the conceptual terms contained in the sentences.
iii.	Extracting and standardizing data values from the text.
iv.	Assigning data values to specific classes of data belonging to a classification system. ?? 
a.	ii, iii and iv
b.	i, ii, iii and iv
c.	i, iii and iv
d.	i, ii and iii

The correct answer is option A: ii, iii and iv. 

To extract information from text, one needs to extract and normalize the conceptual terms contained in the sentences (ii), extract and standardize data values from the text (iii), and assign data values to specific classes of data belonging to a classification system (iv). Translating the text to a preferred language may be necessary in some cases, but it is not always a requirement (i).

Which of the following is correct about big data?

a. Contains structured data only.

b. Usually designed to achieve a specific goal.

c. One set of standard units are used to represent the data.

d. Data are analyzed in incremental steps.

The correct answer is option d. 

Big data refers to extremely large and complex data sets that cannot be processed using traditional data processing methods. One of the key characteristics of big data is that it is analyzed in incremental steps, as it is often too large to be processed all at once. 

Option a is incorrect because big data usually contains both structured and unstructured data. 

Option b is incorrect because big data may not always be designed to achieve a specific goal. 

Option c is incorrect because big data can be represented using a variety of units and formats, depending on the nature of the data and the context in which it is being used.

The following statements are true, except:
a.	Redundancy level is kept high to reduce the indirect cost of the entire system.
b.	Data importance principle is used to decide which data shall be stored and which data shall be discarded.
c.	In big data architecture, professional tools are needed to kept the sensitive information safe.
d.	Data representation helps to build more meaningful structure for computer analysis.

The statement that is NOT true is option a: "Redundancy level is kept high to reduce the indirect cost of the entire system."

Redundancy refers to the duplication of critical components or systems in order to increase the reliability and availability of a system. While redundancy can help to reduce the risk of system failure, it can also be expensive to implement and maintain. Therefore, redundancy is often balanced with cost considerations. In other words, the redundancy level is not kept high to reduce the indirect cost of the entire system.

Option b is true because the data importance principle is used to determine which data to store and which data to discard, based on its value to the organization.

Option c is true because professional tools are used in big data architecture to ensure the security and privacy of sensitive information.

Option d is true because data representation techniques (such as data models, charts, and graphs) help to organize complex data sets and make the data more interpretable and accessible for computer analysis.

The following is false about Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), except:
a.	Multiple replicas are stored in the same DataNode.
b.	NameNode will send heartbeat to DataNode every few seconds to make sure the DataNode is still available.
c.	Client reads data from NameNode after DataNode send the necessary information to NameNode.
d.	There is only one NameNode in HDFS.

The statement that is NOT false is option d: "There is only one NameNode in HDFS."

Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) is a distributed file system that allows large data sets to be stored across multiple machines. It is designed to be highly fault-tolerant and scalable.

Option a is false because HDFS stores multiple replicas of data across different DataNodes for resilience.

Option b is true because NameNode will periodically send heartbeats to DataNodes to ensure that they are still available and functioning.

Option c is true because HDFS is designed to allow clients to read data from different DataNodes, with the necessary information about the data location being provided by the NameNode.

Therefore, the false statement is option a.

Select the statements that are false:

i.   Hadoop distributed file system (HDFS) is designed to run across commodity hardware.

ii.  NameNode actively monitors the number of replicas of a block to make sure the number of replicas is adequate.

iii. NameNode send heartbeats to DataNodes to make sure the DataNodes are functioning.

iv. Client reads data from NameNode after DataNode send the necessary information to NameNode.


a. ii and iv


b. i and ii


c. ii and iii


d. iii and iv

The false statements are i and iv.

i is false because Hadoop distributed file system (HDFS) is specifically designed to run on commodity hardware, to reduce the overall cost of deploying and managing a Hadoop cluster.

iv is false because clients read data directly from the DataNodes, not from the NameNode. The NameNode only provides information about the location of the data.

Therefore, the correct answer is option b: i and iv.

Below are the disadvantages to use traditional file access compared to modern electronic database systems, except:


a.Data manipulation


b.Complexity


c.Accessibility


d.data order

The option that is NOT a disadvantage of using traditional file access compared to modern electronic database systems is option d: "Data order." 

Traditional file access methods are file-based (such as indexed sequential file access and direct file access) where data is stored in a file and accessed using its physical location. Traditional file access methods have the following disadvantages when compared to modern database systems: 

a. Data manipulation is generally limited to basic functions like read, write and delete. Complex data manipulations (like sorting, filtering or searching complex data structures) must be performed manually.

b. They are typically less complex than modern database management systems, which can make them easier to learn and use, but limited in their functionality.

c. They have limited accessibility, especially in multi-user or networked environments, where concurrent access to a file can lead to data inconsistencies or conflicts.

Therefore, option d is the correct answer.

Which of the following is correct about Velocity as one of the V’S in big data characteristics? 
a.	Accurateness of the data.
b.	Meaning of data in different environment.
c.	Analysis of streaming data.
d.	Different forms of data.

The correct answer is option c: "Analysis of streaming data."

Velocity is one of the three V's (along with volume and variety) that defines big data. Velocity refers to the speed at which data is being generated, processed, and analyzed. In today's world, a massive amount of data is generated in real-time, and this data is often unstructured. Big data systems are designed to effectively capture and analyze this data, which may be in the form of streaming data, social media data, or other forms of real-time data.

Therefore, option c is the correct answer, as it directly relates to the way by which big data deals with velocity. Options a, b, and d are not related to velocity, but rather refer to other aspects of big data like accuracy of data, semantic aspects of data, and variety of data types.

The following is false about Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), except:


a.Client reads data from NameNode after DataNode send the necessary information to NameNode.


b.NameNode will send heartbeat to DataNode every few seconds to make sure the DataNode is still available.


c.There is only one NameNode in HDFS.


d.Multiple replicas are stored in the same DataNode.

The false statement is option d: "Multiple replicas are stored in the same DataNode."

In Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), multiple replicas of each data block are stored across multiple DataNodes to ensure data reliability and fault tolerance. Each DataNode stores a subset of data, but not multiple replicas of the same block. This ensures that if a single DataNode fails, the data stored on it can be recovered from the replicas stored on other DataNodes.

Statements a, b, and c are true:

a. Client reads data from DataNodes, not from the NameNode. The NameNode only provides information about the data location.

b. The NameNode sends heartbeats to DataNodes to ensure that they are still available and functioning.

c. HDFS has only one active NameNode at any given time, although multiple standby NameNodes can be configured for fault tolerance.

Therefore, the false statement is option d.

Which of the following is correct about big data?


a.Contains structured data only.


b.Usually designed to achieve a specific goal.


c.One set of standard units are used to represent the data.


d.Data are analyzed in incremental steps.

The correct answer is d: "Data are analyzed in incremental steps."

Big data refers to large, complex datasets that cannot be processed using traditional data processing methods. The term "big" refers not only to the size of the data, but also to the high velocity (speed of generation), variety (different types of data), and veracity (quality and reliability) of the data.

Analysis of big data typically involves breaking down the dataset into smaller, manageable chunks and processing them in incremental steps rather than analyzing the entire dataset at once. This incremental processing is necessary due to the large size and complexity of the data. 

Option a is incorrect because big data often includes both structured and unstructured data.

Option b is incorrect because big data may not always be designed to achieve a specific goal; it may be analyzed for exploratory or descriptive purposes.

Option c is incorrect because big data often involves data from multiple sources, each with their own units and formats.

Therefore, the correct answer is d.

Below are the disadvantages to use traditional file access compared to modern electronic database systems, except:
a.	Complexity
b.	Data manipulation
c.	Data order
d.	Accessibility

The disadvantage that is NOT typically associated with traditional file access compared to modern electronic database systems is option c, "Data order."

Traditional file access methods use file-based approaches such as indexed sequential file access and direct file access, where the data is stored in a file and accessed using its physical location. These methods have several disadvantages compared to modern electronic database systems:

a. Complexity: Traditional file access methods are typically less complex than modern databases, which can limit functionality but also make them more user-friendly.

b. Data manipulation: Data manipulation using traditional file systems is usually limited to basic functions (read, write, delete) and complex data manipulations are usually performed manually, which can be error-prone and time-consuming.

d. Accessibility: Traditional file access methods are often limited in their accessibility, especially in distributed/networked environments where concurrent access to a file can lead to data inconsistencies or conflicts.

Option c, "Data order," is not a typical disadvantage associated with traditional file access. 

Therefore, the correct answer is option c.

The following is true about classification, except:


a.The members of classes may be highly similar to one another, but their similarities result from their membership in the same class, and not the other way around.


b.The classes of the hierarchy have a set of properties or rules that extend to every member of the class and to all of the subclasses of the class.


c.In a hierarchical classification, each subclass may have no more than one parent class.


d.Every instance belongs to more than one class.

The true statement that is NOT correct is option d: "Every instance belongs to more than one class."

Classification is the process of organizing information into different categories or classes based on common characteristics or properties. It is used extensively in data science and machine learning for tasks like sentiment analysis, text classification, and image recognition.

Options a, b, and c are true:

a. In a classification hierarchy, members of classes may be highly similar to one another, but their similarities result from their membership in the same class, not the other way around.

b. The classes in a hierarchical classification have a set of properties or rules that extend to every member of the class and to all of the subclasses of the class.

c. In a hierarchical classification, each subclass must have exactly one parent class.

Option d is false, because each instance can only belong to one class in most classification systems. However, in some more complex classification systems (such as overlapping or fuzzy classification systems), an instance may belong to multiple classes depending on the specific criteria being used to define those classes.

Therefore, the correct answer is option d.

The following statements are true, except:


a.
Redundancy level is kept high to reduce the indirect cost of the entire system.


b.
Data importance principle is used to decide which data shall be stored and which data shall be discarded.


c.
Data representation helps to build more meaningful structure for computer analysis.


d.
In big data architecture, professional tools are needed to kept the sensitive information safe.

The true statement that is NOT correct is option a: "Redundancy level is kept high to reduce the indirect cost of the entire system."

Redundancy refers to the duplication of critical components or systems in order to increase the reliability and availability of a system. While redundancy can help to reduce the risk of system failure, it can also be expensive to implement and maintain. Therefore, redundancy is often balanced with cost considerations. In other words, the redundancy level is not kept high to reduce the indirect cost of the entire system.

Option b is true because the data importance principle is used to determine which data to store and which data to discard, based on its value to the organization.

Option c is true because data representation techniques (such as data models, charts, and graphs) help to organize complex data sets and make the data more interpretable and accessible for computer analysis.

Option d is true because professional tools and techniques are needed in big data architecture to ensure the security and privacy of sensitive information.

Therefore, the correct answer is option a.

Which of the following is correct about Velocity as one of the V’s in big data characteristics?


a.
Meaning of data in different environment.


b.
Accurateness of the data.


c.
Analysis of streaming data.


d.
Different forms of data.

The correct answer is option c: "Analysis of streaming data."

Velocity is one of the three V's (along with volume and variety) that define big data. It refers to the speed at which data is generated, processed, and analyzed. In today's world, a massive amount of data is generated in real-time, and this data is often unstructured. Big data systems are designed to effectively capture, process and analyze this data in real-time, which may be in the form of streaming data, social media data, or other forms of real-time data.

Option a, "Meaning of data in different environment," is not related to velocity, but refers to the semantic aspects of big data.

Option b, "Accurateness of data," is not directly related to velocity, but rather refers to the quality and reliability of big data.

Option d, "Different forms of data," is related to one of the aspects of big data which is variety.

Therefore, the correct answer is option c.

Select the Hadoop tools that is used for data access:

i.  YARN

ii. Hive

iii. Kafka

iv. Ambari


a.
iii and iv


b.
ii and iii


c.
ii and iv


d.
i and ii

The correct answer is option c: "ii and iv - Hive and Ambari."

Hadoop is a framework that allows for the distributed processing of large datasets across clusters of computers. It consists of several tools and technologies that are used for different aspects of big data processing and analysis.

YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator) is a cluster management technology in Hadoop that is used to manage resources and schedule tasks across the cluster.

Hive is a data warehousing tool in Hadoop that provides a SQL-like query language for data analysis. It is used to manage and query large datasets stored in Hadoop.

Kafka is a distributed messaging system that is used for real-time data streaming and processing.

Ambari is a cluster management and monitoring tool in Hadoop that is used to provision, manage, and monitor Hadoop clusters.

Therefore, the correct answer is option c: "ii and iv - Hive and Ambari."

To extract information from text, it is necessary to design structure for it. This may involve:

i.   translating the text to a preferred language

ii.  extracting and normalizing the conceptual terms contained in the sentences

iii. extracting and standardizing data values from the text

iv. assigning data values to specific classes of data belonging to a classification system

The correct answer is option a: "ii, iii, and iv - extracting and normalizing the conceptual terms contained in the sentences; extracting and standardizing data values from the text; and assigning data values to specific classes of data belonging to a classification system."

To extract information from text, it is necessary to design structure for it. This involves a number of steps including:

i. Pre-processing the text to remove noise, punctuation, and stop words.

ii. Extracting and normalizing the conceptual terms contained in the sentences, such as nouns and verbs.

iii. Extracting and standardizing data values from the text, such as dates, times, and quantities.

iv. Assigning data values to specific classes of data belonging to a classification system, such as labeling text as positive or negative sentiment, assigning topics or categories to documents, or identifying named entities like people, places, and organizations.

Option a is incorrect because while translating the text to a preferred language may be necessary in some cases, it is not always required for information extraction.

Therefore, the correct answer is option b.

Select the statement that are false:
i.	Hadoop distributed file system (HDFS) is designed to run across commodity hardware.
ii.	NameNode actively monitors the number of replicas of a block to make sure the number of replicas is adequate.
iii.	NameNode send heartbeats to DataNodes to make sure the DataNodes are functioning.
iv.	Client reads data from NameNode after DataNode send the necessary information to NameNode.
a.	iii and iv
b.	ii and iii
c.	i and ii
d.	ii and iv

The false statement is option d: "ii and iv".
